---
title: "Web Technology"
subtitle: "BMC201 • MCA Semester 2 • 2025-26"
details: |
  Prashant Kumar Nag<br>Galgotias College of Engineering & Technology
sidebar: false
toc: false
page-layout: full
format:
  html:
    template-partials:
      - html/home/title-block.html
course-logo: /files/favicon-512.png
---

::: {.home}

::: {.grid .course-details}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-sm-6 .g-col-md-4}
### Instructor

- {{< fa user >}} &nbsp; [Dr. Prashant Kumar Nag](https://prashantnag.com)
- {{< fa university >}} &nbsp; Department of Computer Applications
- {{< fa envelope >}} &nbsp; <a href="mailto:prashantkumar.nag@galgotiacollege.edu">prashantkumar.nag@galgotiacollege.edu</a>
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-sm-6 .g-col-md-4}
### Course Details

- {{< fa calendar >}} &nbsp; MCA Semester 2
- {{< fa calendar-alt >}} &nbsp; 2025-26
- {{< fa university >}} &nbsp; Galgotias College of Engineering & Technology
- {{< fa book >}} &nbsp; Course Code: BMC201
- {{< fa gears >}} &nbsp; Credits: 4 (3-1-0)
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4 .contact-policy}
### Quick Links

- [{{< fa book-open >}}  Syllabus](/syllabus.qmd)
- [{{< fa calendar-days >}}  Schedule](/schedule.qmd)
- [{{< fa laptop-code >}}  Lab Manuals](/labs/index.qmd)
- [{{< fa file-lines >}}  Assignments](/assignment/index.qmd)
:::

::::

:::

## Welcome to Web Technology! {.mb-5}

This course provides a **comprehensive introduction to web technologies**, covering both front-end and back-end development. You'll learn to create dynamic, interactive websites and web applications using **HTML, CSS, JavaScript, and server-side programming**.

**Course Duration:** January 26 - May 3, 2026 | **Credits:** 4 (3-1-0) | **Code:** BMC201

---

## 📚 What You'll Learn

::: {.grid .mb-4}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
### Front-End Development

- **HTML5 & CSS3**: Build modern, responsive web pages with semantic markup
- **JavaScript**: Add interactivity and dynamic behavior to your websites
- **User Interface**: Create engaging and functional UI designs
- **Responsive Design**: Make websites work on all devices

:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
### Back-End Development

- **Server-Side Programming**: JSP, Servlets, Spring Framework, Spring Boot
- **Databases**: Data management and SQL fundamentals
- **Web Servers**: Configuration, deployment, and management
- **APIs**: Build RESTful web services

:::

::: {.g-col-12}
### Best Practices & Architecture

- **Client-Server Architecture**: Understand how the web works at a fundamental level
- **Web Standards**: Industry-standard practices and conventions
- **Security Basics**: Fundamental security concepts for web applications
- **Performance**: Optimization techniques for web applications

:::

:::

---

## 📅 Course Structure

**12 Weeks with Consistent Weekly Pattern:**

- **4 Classes per week** - Interactive lectures, slides, and reference materials
- **1 Lab per week** - 3-hour hands-on lab sessions aligned with lecture topics
- **Self-paced materials** - Additional readings and resources available anytime

### Unit-by-Unit Breakdown

| Unit | Topics | Weeks |
|------|--------|-------|
| **Unit I** | Web Page Designing (HTML, CSS, Bootstrap) | 1-2 |
| **Unit II** | JavaScript & DOM Manipulation | 3-5 |
| **Unit III** | Web Applications (JSP & Servlets) | 8-9 |
| **Unit IV** | Spring Framework Basics | 10 |
| **Unit V** | Spring Boot & RESTful Web Services | 11-12 |
| **Mid-Semester** | Revision & Examination | 6-7 |

---

## 🎯 Learning Outcomes

By the end of this course, you will be able to:

1. Design and develop static and dynamic web pages using HTML, CSS, and JavaScript
2. Understand and implement client-server architecture
3. Configure and manage web servers effectively
4. Apply industry best practices in web development
5. Build complete web applications from scratch
6. Debug and troubleshoot web applications professionally

---

## 📋 Course Materials

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
### Schedule & Content

- **[Complete Schedule](/schedule.qmd)** - Week-by-week breakdown with topics and dates
- **[Lecture Materials](/content/index.qmd)** - Slides, references, and learning materials
- **[Assignments](/assignment/index.qmd)** - 4 major projects throughout the semester
- **[Lab Exercises](/labs/index.qmd)** - 9 hands-on labs (one per week)

:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
### Additional Resources

- **[Syllabus](/syllabus.qmd)** - Course policies and guidelines
- **[Reference Materials](/resource/index.qmd)** - Tools, references, and learning materials
- **[AKTU Compliance](/AKTU_COMPLIANCE_UPDATES.md)** - Curriculum alignment
- **[Getting Started](/GETTING_STARTED.md)** - Setup and onboarding guide

:::

:::

---

## Prerequisites

- Basic understanding of computers and the internet
- Familiarity with any programming language (helpful but not required)
- Commitment to learning web development

---

## ❓ Getting Help

::: {.callout-note icon=false appearance="simple"}
### Need Assistance?

**Email:** [prashantkumar.nag@galgotiacollege.edu](mailto:prashantkumar.nag@galgotiacollege.edu)

**Office:** Department of Computer Applications, Galgotias College of Engineering & Technology

**Response Time:** 24-48 hours during weekdays

For quick answers, check the [Resources](/resource/index.qmd) page or relevant [Lab Manual](/labs/index.qmd) first.
:::
